div.head: The Minister in Sweden (Morris) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1541. Minister of Foreign Affairs yesterday informed French Minister that as a result of negotiations between Vorovski, Bolshevik representative here, Russian soldiers, Red and White Guards, and the commander of Swedish expedition to the Aland Islands, it had been agreed that all three groups would leave the islands on Swedish ships on condition that Sweden would occupy islands with sufficient force to protect population, who do not wish to take sides in present Finnish struggle, but claim peace and protection. Swedish Government therefore decided to send, and did send yesterday, additional expedition composed of one infantry and one machine-gun company which with the force already there would amount to about 500 men. Commander has instructions not to land his troops before the three groups are ready to leave islands so as to avoid complications.

Minister of Foreign Affairs added that he desired to inform French and British Ministers of this, and to declare at the same time that this action was not to be considered as the beginning of a permanent occupation as the Swedish Government was still of the opinion that the Aland Islands question should be solved by negotiations between England, France, and Finland and if these negotiations led to no result, then at the peace conference.
